title: Reimagine focus and contrast for compact legend + list widgets to meet VPAT inputs
preview: Background: During the latest VPAT review we surfaced inconsistent keyboard focus behaviors and a handful of contrast regressions in compact list rows and chart legend items. These components are reused across dashboards (dense tables, compact sidebars, micro-visualizations) and are frequently embedded in third-part...
text: Background: During the latest VPAT review we surfaced inconsistent keyboard focus behaviors and a handful of contrast regressions in compact list rows and chart legend items. These components are reused across dashboards (dense tables, compact sidebars, micro-visualizations) and are frequently embedded in third-party dashboards via the SDK. The goal of this ticket is to deliver a small-surface redesign that: 1) standardizes focusable roles and tab order for compact list rows and legend chips, 2) stabilizes visual focus styles that work at compact densities without increasing layout shift, and 3) remaps contrast tokens to ensure >=4.5:1 for primary interactive elements and >=3:1 for supplemental text when density constraints apply.

title: Table folding mode and proportional resize for very wide datasets
preview: Introduce a 'folding' interaction and a proportional column-resize algorithm for the virtualized table component to improve usability on very wide datasets. Folding groups low-priority/overflow columns into a single collapsible pane while keeping critical columns visible; proportional resize smoothly allocates width...
text: Introduce a 'folding' interaction and a proportional column-resize algorithm for the virtualized table component to improve usability on very wide datasets. Folding groups low-priority/overflow columns into a single collapsible pane while keeping critical columns visible; proportional resize smoothly allocates width deltas across non-locked columns respecting min/max constraints and user pinning. This ticket covers design exploration, accessible interactions, animation guidelines, and handoff artifacts for engineering implementation.

title: Adaptive column freezing, fluid resize gestures, and filter affordances for large virtualized tables
preview: Purpose: deliver a cohesive set of UX patterns and interactions for very large virtualized tables that make it easier for users to keep context, manipulate columns, and discover sort & filter controls without regressing performance or accessibility. Scope covers: column freezing (pin left/right), fluid pointer + key...
text: Purpose: deliver a cohesive set of UX patterns and interactions for very large virtualized tables that make it easier for users to keep context, manipulate columns, and discover sort & filter controls without regressing performance or accessibility. Scope covers: column freezing (pin left/right), fluid pointer + keyboard resize gestures that respect live virtualization, persistent per-column filter affordances, and per-column density overrides. Does not include server-side filtering backends or query design; those will be separate infra tickets.

title: Traffic Orchestrator: Visual Flow for Multi-Variant Rollouts
preview: Design a compact, scannable visual flow for Redwood Console that lets users create, preview, and manage multi-variant rollouts (canary + A/B combinations) with clear rollback affordances and temporal ramps. The goal is a single-pane orchestration surface that: 1) makes intent explicit (who gets what % of traffic and...
text: Design a compact, scannable visual flow for Redwood Console that lets users create, preview, and manage multi-variant rollouts (canary + A/B combinations) with clear rollback affordances and temporal ramps. The goal is a single-pane orchestration surface that: 1) makes intent explicit (who gets what % of traffic and when), 2) surfaces safety signals and automatic fallback options, 3) enables quick edits and fast rollbacks without navigating between multiple modals, and 4) preserves compatibility with existing runbook/playbook concepts. This ticket covers low-fidelity flow maps, mid-fidelity interactive prototype (Figma + clickable timeline), and a feedback pass with Eng/PM including trade-offs for complexity vs. discoverability.

title: Table Personalization: Keyboard-first Compact Views + Saved Column Sets
preview:
Summary:
Introduce a keyboard-first compact view and saved column-set personalization for the virtualized Table component. This ticket covers the design deliverables and UX decisions required to let power users quickly switch between dense/compact modes, persist column visibility + order as named views, and operate ...
text:
Summary:
Introduce a keyboard-first compact view and saved column-set personalization for the virtualized Table component. This ticket covers the design deliverables and UX decisions required to let power users quickly switch between dense/compact modes, persist column visibility + order as named views, and operate all controls via keyboard and screen-reader-friendly announcements.

Background / Problem:
Our current table has several competing needs: teams want high-density views for large result sets, keyboard-oriented power users expect fast toggles without mouse interaction, and admins want the ability to store frequently-used column configurations as named views. Today these are partly implemented as ad hoc features in product code, but there is no cohesive design system pattern or accessible keyboard-first flow. This leads to inconsistent affordances across products, duplicated e...

title: Compact viewport sampler and ARIA summaries for embedded time-series
preview: Problem: Compact time-series thumbnails (list/table rows, sidecar cards) are rendering full-resolution paths and verbose tooltips which increases CPU work, memory churn, and creates noisy screen reader output when many charts are present. We need a design solution that reduces render cost, keeps visual fidelity for ...
text: Problem: Compact time-series thumbnails (list/table rows, sidecar cards) are rendering full-resolution paths and verbose tooltips which increases CPU work, memory churn, and creates noisy screen reader output when many charts are present. We need a design solution that reduces render cost, keeps visual fidelity for trend/peak detection, and produces concise, stable ARIA-friendly summaries that don't flood assistive technology.

title: Compact query builder + embeddings inspector: component library & Figma variant mapping
preview: Create a compact, reusable component set and variant mapping in Figma for the query builder control and an 'Embeddings Inspector' panel used on cost & diagnostics dashboards. Deliver a tokens-first spec (spacing, elevation, color states, motion) and a complete mapping between design variants and React props so engin...
text: Create a compact, reusable component set and variant mapping in Figma for the query builder control and an 'Embeddings Inspector' panel used on cost & diagnostics dashboards. Deliver a tokens-first spec (spacing, elevation, color states, motion) and a complete mapping between design variants and React props so engineering can implement a single, accessible component library across Hosted/Dedicated/Private console surfaces.

title: Avatar border and sparkline visibility in low-light theme
preview: Audit and implement token mapping and component overrides to improve perceived contrast and visual clarity for small surface components (avatar borders, compact controls, sparklines) in the low-light theme. Current issues: 1) 1px avatar borders disappear against deep surfaces at small sizes; 2) sparklines lose reada...
text: Audit and implement token mapping and component overrides to improve perceived contrast and visual clarity for small surface components (avatar borders, compact controls, sparklines) in the low-light theme. Current issues: 1) 1px avatar borders disappear against deep surfaces at small sizes; 2) sparklines lose readability when tokenized stroke colors are mapped directly from light theme swatches; 3) compact table row separators and dense control focus rings have inconsistent foreground priority. Deliverables: token adjustments, new fallback rules, component-level overrides for Avatar, Sparkline, TableRow, and CompactButton, automated visual tests, and a brief dev handoff document for runtime engineers on mapping rules and theming flags.

title: Embeddings & Rerank Quickstart — hero assets and stepwise flow visuals
preview: Design and produce a compact set of onboarding visuals for the Embeddings + Rerank quickstart that ship with the 2026.03 console release. Deliverables include a hero illustration for the quickstart landing, a clear pipeline diagram (two variants: developer-focused and product-focused), annotated sample output cards ...
text: Design and produce a compact set of onboarding visuals for the Embeddings + Rerank quickstart that ship with the 2026.03 console release. Deliverables include a hero illustration for the quickstart landing, a clear pipeline diagram (two variants: developer-focused and product-focused), annotated sample output cards (text + score + progression of rerank), and 3 responsive screenshots for docs and in-console carousel. Visuals should emphasize the pipeline: embed -> index -> query -> rerank -> serve and make cost/latency tradeoffs explicit in the product-focused diagram.

title: Refactor onboarding micro-assets for dark theme contrast and glyph clarity
preview: Goal: Complete an accessibility-first dark-mode pass on all onboarding micro-assets and quickstart screenshots so first-time users (including low-vision and high-contrast needs) can onboard without friction. Scope covers: SVG icon set used across onboarding flows, inline illustrative assets (cards/tiles), CTA/button...
text:
Goal: Complete an accessibility-first dark-mode pass on all onboarding micro-assets and quickstart screenshots so first-time users (including low-vision and high-contrast needs) can onboard without friction. Scope covers: SVG icon set used across onboarding flows, inline illustrative assets (cards/tiles), CTA/button states in welcome & setup flows, and canonical quickstart product screenshots required for release notes and docs.

Key constraints / targets:
- Contrast targets: meet WCAG AA for body text and UI elements (minimum 4.5:1) and AA for large text/icons where applicable (3:1 allowed for large). Aim for 3:1 minimum for decorative glyphs but prefer 4.5:1 for functional icons.
- Iconography: simplify strokes and increase x-height-equivalent metrics for glyphs rendered at 16px and 24px. Use consistent 1.5px stroke on light, 2px effective dark stroke (via inner-outline technique) t...

title: Chart layer compositing and offscreen canvas strategy for long-series performance
preview: Goal: Define a design + implementation guide for compositing time-series chart layers using offscreen canvases and a lightweight DOM overlay so that charts with tens of thousands of points remain interactive on mid-range machines. This ticket covers visual layering rules, accessible fallback semantics, interactions ...
text: Goal: Define a design + implementation guide for compositing time-series chart layers using offscreen canvases and a lightweight DOM overlay so that charts with tens of thousands of points remain interactive on mid-range machines. This ticket covers visual layering rules, accessible fallback semantics, interactions with legends/tooltips, and token implications for stroke/fill/hover states. We must provide prescriptive patterns the engineering team can follow (offscreen canvas pooling, ImageBitmap reuse, layer invalidation signals) and ensure ARIA semantics are not lost when rendering to canvas.

title: Starter Threshold Entrymap: compact onboarding visual system
preview: Design and deliver a compact, accessible 'entrymap' visual system for the Quickstart landing to replace the current full-width hero. Focus: condensed hero alternative for compact viewports, staged step-rail diagram, and a set of product screenshots with contextual callouts. Deliverables include Figma file, export-re...
text: Design and deliver a compact, accessible 'entrymap' visual system for the Quickstart landing to replace the current full-width hero. Focus: condensed hero alternative for compact viewports, staged step-rail diagram, and a set of product screenshots with contextual callouts. Deliverables include Figma file, export-ready SVG/PNG artboards, production-ready microcopy, and a screenshot capture runbook for engineering to reproduce.

title: Legend condensation mode for high-density time-series: SR summarization and DOM reduction
preview: Problem: Large time-series graphs with dozens to hundreds of series overload the visual legend and the DOM, causing slow render, poor keyboard navigation, and noisy screen-reader output. Existing approaches either virtualize the full list (loses context) or truncate visually but still expose many nodes to assistive ...
text:
Problem: Large time-series graphs with dozens to hundreds of series overload the visual legend and the DOM, causing slow render, poor keyboard navigation, and noisy screen-reader output. Existing approaches either virtualize the full list (loses context) or truncate visually but still expose many nodes to assistive tech.

Goal: Design a compact ‘condensation mode’ for legends that reduces DOM nodes, provides semantic summarization for screen readers, preserves quick visual discovery for sighted users, and allows progressive expansion. The pattern should be configurable by product and work across canvas- and SVG-backed charts.

Proposed approach:
- Introduce three presentation tiers: concise summary (0–6 visible legend items + ellipses), grouped micro-rows (category buckets with counts), and full-expansion on demand.
- Render the concise UI as a single semantic container for assistive ...

title: Component hierarchy rationalization and constraint preservation for Figma library
preview:
Overview:
This ticket organizes and repairs several systemic issues discovered during the February audit of the Figma UI kit: inconsistent component hierarchies, accidental overrides that break auto-layout constraints, and token mismatches across buttons, tables, and chart components. The goal is to rationalize comp...
text:
Overview:
This ticket organizes and repairs several systemic issues discovered during the February audit of the Figma UI kit: inconsistent component hierarchies, accidental overrides that break auto-layout constraints, and token mismatches across buttons, tables, and chart components. The goal is to rationalize component nesting, preserve constraints (so consumers do not break), consolidate token aliases, and create a non-destructive publish flow with a clear deprecation schedule for old variants.

Background & motivation:
- Multiple teams reported layout regressions after we published the last library patch: primary buttons lost horizontal padding when consumers resized frames, and table cell templates sometimes clipped content due to nested fixed-size frames.
- Current variant naming is inconsistent (e.g., "Primary/Default" vs "Button / Primary"), which makes programmatic mapping an...

title: Traffic Split Visual Authoring + Rollback Flow
preview: Design an end-to-end visual authoring experience for defining traffic splits across model variants and rollout phases, including a clear rollback path and integrated cost/latency preview. This ticket covers high-fidelity mockups, clickable prototype, and a short usability test script for PM/ENG review.
text: Design an end-to-end visual authoring experience for defining traffic splits across model variants and rollout phases, including a clear rollback path and integrated cost/latency preview. This ticket covers high-fidelity mockups, clickable prototype, and a short usability test script for PM/ENG review.

title: Mini-dashboard widgets: keyboard-first flow, RTL mirroring, and adaptive contrast audit
preview: Audit and prototype updates for compact dashboard widgets used in Cost Explorer and Rollouts pages. Goal: make mini-widgets keyboard-first (tab/arrow navigation), ensure correct RTL mirroring for collapsed widgets, and validate adaptive color token usage across contrast themes and locale-specific number formats. Thi...
text: Audit and prototype updates for compact dashboard widgets used in Cost Explorer and Rollouts pages. Goal: make mini-widgets keyboard-first (tab/arrow navigation), ensure correct RTL mirroring for collapsed widgets, and validate adaptive color token usage across contrast themes and locale-specific number formats. This ticket captures the design changes, prototype links, accessibility testing plan, and rollout checklist for handoff to engineering.

title: Reduce loading jitter: adaptive spinner, text-preserving states, and theme-aware motion
preview: Problem: Users and telemetry show that button interactions feel unstable when network latency varies: the loading spinner appears too quickly on short waits, causes text shift when replacing label content, and different themes (dark vs light/high-contrast) expose poor contrast or invisible spinners. Additionally, re...
text: Problem: Users and telemetry show that button interactions feel unstable when network latency varies: the loading spinner appears too quickly on short waits, causes text shift when replacing label content, and different themes (dark vs light/high-contrast) expose poor contrast or invisible spinners. Additionally, reduced-motion and a11y settings are not respected consistently across icon-only buttons. This ticket defines a design spec for adaptive loading affordances that preserve label width, respect reduced-motion and color tokens, and provide an escape path to cancel long waits. Goals: (1) avoid visual jitter by deferring and crossfading loading indicators rather than snapping; (2) maintain layout stability by reserving label width and optionally overlaying spinner without replacing text; (3) ensure spinner/icon contrast meets WCAG in all themes; (4) add a micro-interaction pattern...

title: Tenant Admin Role Wizard and Bulk Member Invite UX
preview:
Summary:
Design a guided 'Role Wizard' and an improved bulk-member-invite UX for Tenant Admins as part of RBAC v2. The goal is to make creation, review, and assignment of roles safer and faster while reducing accidental over-permissioning. The experience must integrate permission templates, SSO/SAML group mappings, ...
text:
Summary:
Design a guided 'Role Wizard' and an improved bulk-member-invite UX for Tenant Admins as part of RBAC v2. The goal is to make creation, review, and assignment of roles safer and faster while reducing accidental over-permissioning. The experience must integrate permission templates, SSO/SAML group mappings, an audit-preview step, and a simulated permission impact checker tied to audit logs.

Goals:
1) Enable tenant admins to create new roles via a step-by-step wizard that enforces policy guardrails (cannot grant higher privileges than the creator unless explicitly approved).
2) Provide curated role templates (read-only, developer, ops, billing) and allow copying/editing for quick onboarding.
3) Support granular permission toggles with contextual help (what the permission allows, typical risks, linked audit events).
4) Offer a bulk-invite flow that accepts CSV, previews assignm...

title: Decentralized Maintenance & Autonomy Playbook for Tokens and UI Primitives
preview: Goal: establish a lightweight, decentralized maintenance playbook that empowers product teams to own component and token life-cycles (tokens, buttons, tables, charts) while preserving system-wide consistency and safe deprecation paths. This ticket captures the operational flow, review checklist, RFC gating rules, te...
text: Goal: establish a lightweight, decentralized maintenance playbook that empowers product teams to own component and token life-cycles (tokens, buttons, tables, charts) while preserving system-wide consistency and safe deprecation paths. This ticket captures the operational flow, review checklist, RFC gating rules, telemetry signals for deprecation, and communication templates required to roll out the process across Design, Eng, and PM.

title: Reusable spark-band component with ARIA summary and lazy aggregation
preview:
Problem: Several dashboards rely on bespoke sparkline and band components implemented inconsistently across products. This leads to duplicated logic, accessibility variance (different aria summaries), and performance regressions when many small charts render on the same page.

Goal: Design a single, reusable 'spark-...
text:
Problem: Several dashboards rely on bespoke sparkline and band components implemented inconsistently across products. This leads to duplicated logic, accessibility variance (different aria summaries), and performance regressions when many small charts render on the same page.

Goal: Design a single, reusable 'spark-band' primitive optimized for dense time-series displays. It should: (1) provide a concise ARIA summary for screen readers, (2) expose a small API for visual and interaction variants (line, area, band, micro-bar), (3) support lazy aggregation/decimation for high-point series, (4) adopt tokens for color/spacing, and (5) include a Canvas-first implementation with an accessible DOM fallback for legacy browsers/screen readers.

title: Adoption checklist + real-world snippets for theming, tokens, and component migrations
preview: Create a practical adoption checklist and a gallery of short, copy-pasteable examples that demonstrate: token substitution patterns, button theming (primary/secondary/ghost), table column density and responsive behavior, and chart color/legend best practices. The deliverable is a set of docs pages and a Storybook co...
text: Create a practical adoption checklist and a gallery of short, copy-pasteable examples that demonstrate: token substitution patterns, button theming (primary/secondary/ghost), table column density and responsive behavior, and chart color/legend best practices. The deliverable is a set of docs pages and a Storybook collection with linting rules and migration notes for engineers and designers to reduce ambiguity during upgrades.

title: Contrast-led token rebake and compact UI guidelines
preview: Background: As Redwood expands dedicated and private deployments, component density and accessibility reports show a recurring set of issues in compact layouts: insufficient contrast on subtle surface ramps, clipped chart axis labels, inconsistent button radii across size variants, and shadow depth leakage at small ...
text:
Background: As Redwood expands dedicated and private deployments, component density and accessibility reports show a recurring set of issues in compact layouts: insufficient contrast on subtle surface ramps, clipped chart axis labels, inconsistent button radii across size variants, and shadow depth leakage at small elevation values. This ticket scopes a focused "rebake" of semantic tokens with a contrast-first lens and a companion compact-mode guidelines doc for components (buttons, tables, charts, form elements).

Goal: Produce an updated token map (color ramps, type scale, spacing/radius, elevation) with clear migration guidance and a small set of component-level adjustments to land in the next console release. Emphasize WCAG AA compliance for normal text and AA/AAA where feasible for UI controls, while preserving the existing visual language and minimizing churn for product surface...

title: Miniature Column Summaries, Ambient Density and Coalescing Columns for Virtual Table
preview:
Problem: Our virtualized table works well for wide datasets but in compact/dense modes users lose contextual signals (what columns were trimmed, quick totals, and important cell summaries). Engineering wants a UX that reduces horizontal space pressure without requiring modal previews or persistent column pinning.

G...
text:
Problem: Our virtualized table works well for wide datasets but in compact/dense modes users lose contextual signals (what columns were trimmed, quick totals, and important cell summaries). Engineering wants a UX that reduces horizontal space pressure without requiring modal previews or persistent column pinning.

Goal: Design a compact-first pattern that preserves discoverability and skim-ability in high-density table views by introducing three complementary concepts: 1) miniature column summaries that condense key metrics into the header, 2) ambient density tokens that harmonize spacing/padding/typography across density modes, and 3) a coalescing behavior that groups low-priority columns into an inline summary cell that can be expanded on demand. The solution must be accessible, implementable within virtualization constraints, and provide clear telemetry hooks.

Proposed solution (h...

title: Consolidate surface tokens and compact variants for dense dataviz kit
preview: Problem: The DataViz surface kit (buttons, table rows, chart legends, small density cards) has grown a high-entropy set of visual-only variants and duplicated surface tokens leading to publish failures, inconsistent runtime spacing, and bloated instance counts. Goal: Reduce variant entropy by collapsing visually red...
text: Problem: The DataViz surface kit (buttons, table rows, chart legends, small density cards) has grown a high-entropy set of visual-only variants and duplicated surface tokens leading to publish failures, inconsistent runtime spacing, and bloated instance counts. Goal: Reduce variant entropy by collapsing visually redundant variants into token-driven states, normalize token names to the code spec, and add a non-destructive publish pipeline with preflight checks and a targeted instance migration plan. This work should enable predictable library publishes, make auto-layout constraints resilient for responsive breakpoints, and reduce downstream engineering churn when mapping Figma tokens to CSS/Design tokens. Deliverables: 1) Audit spreadsheet of all surface tokens and variants across DataViz libraries. 2) Variant compression plan that documents which visual differences become tokens vs wh...

title: Compact interactive example cards and annotated diff overlays for embeddings + rerank quickstart
preview: Design and produce a compact set of interactive example cards and annotated diff overlays that will be used in the Embeddings & Rerank quickstart docs. The goal is a digestible, mobile-friendly visual module that demonstrates: 1) building an embedding index, 2) running a naive similarity retrieval, 3) applying reran...
text: Design and produce a compact set of interactive example cards and annotated diff overlays that will be used in the Embeddings & Rerank quickstart docs. The goal is a digestible, mobile-friendly visual module that demonstrates: 1) building an embedding index, 2) running a naive similarity retrieval, 3) applying rerank to reorder results, and 4) visualizing the rerank score delta (before/after) with callouts for why items moved. Must be small enough for hero placement on the quickstart page and also export as separate assets for the docs and Console walkthrough.

title: Introduce contextual surface maps and atomic spacing grid
preview: Create a new token surface model (contextual surface maps) and an atomic spacing grid to improve density consistency across buttons, tables, and charts. This ticket covers: 1) definition of contextual surface layers (background, card, overlay, inset) with micro-elevation steps tuned for dense analytics surfaces; 2) ...
text: Create a new token surface model (contextual surface maps) and an atomic spacing grid to improve density consistency across buttons, tables, and charts. This ticket covers: 1) definition of contextual surface layers (background, card, overlay, inset) with micro-elevation steps tuned for dense analytics surfaces; 2) an atomic spacing/ radius grid that supports compact and default component modes via interpolation; 3) palette accents for chart geometry that maintain contrast at small sizes. Deliverables include token spec, Figma token file, migration guidance for components, and a short rollout plan with compatibility aliases.

title: Prefill Presets Canvas & Risk-Aware Configuration Flow
preview: Design a compact, canvas-style configuration experience that lets engineers pick, preview, and tune prefill and batching presets with embedded risk-aware guardrails and inline explainers. This flows aims to reduce cognitive load when teams adopt batching/caching optimizations by surfacing trade-offs (latency vs cost...
text: Design a compact, canvas-style configuration experience that lets engineers pick, preview, and tune prefill and batching presets with embedded risk-aware guardrails and inline explainers. This flows aims to reduce cognitive load when teams adopt batching/caching optimizations by surfacing trade-offs (latency vs cost vs reliability), simulating effects on a representative workload, and providing safe defaults and rollback guidance for production rollouts.

title: Starter Lattice — onboarding snapshot pack
preview: Create a compact, exportable asset pack for the Console first-run onboarding flow tailored for three personas (Developer, Platform Admin, Security Officer). Deliverables include: modular checklist tiles, three empty-state illustrations with contextual CTAs, four product screenshot compositions (desktop + narrow), tw...
text: Create a compact, exportable asset pack for the Console first-run onboarding flow tailored for three personas (Developer, Platform Admin, Security Officer). Deliverables include: modular checklist tiles, three empty-state illustrations with contextual CTAs, four product screenshot compositions (desktop + narrow), two lightweight spotlight animations (Lottie JSON), and a microcopy sheet with intl-ready strings. Assets must match console-2026.03 visual tokens and be optimized for performance in the web onboarding tour (SVGs for illustrations, 2x PNGs for screenshots).

title: Performance Studio: Per-Model and Per-Route Token Flow + Cost Layering Spec
preview: Goal: Define a compact, scannable ‘Performance Studio’ view that surfaces per-model and per-route token flow, latency distribution, throughput, and cost layers in a single composable canvas. Deliverables: 1) chart specs for Token Stream (waterfall + micro-lanes), Token Rate (sparklines + histogram), Latency Distribu...
text: Goal: Define a compact, scannable ‘Performance Studio’ view that surfaces per-model and per-route token flow, latency distribution, throughput, and cost layers in a single composable canvas. Deliverables: 1) chart specs for Token Stream (waterfall + micro-lanes), Token Rate (sparklines + histogram), Latency Distribution (p99/p95/p50 bands + kernel overlay), Throughput heatmap, and Cost Layering (stacked area that maps batching/quantization/cache savings to monetary units); 2) interaction patterns (time windowing, zoom, trace linkouts, model variant toggle); 3) accessibility and performance constraints; 4) annotated mockups and a Figma prototype for designer/PM/eng review. Scope: Per-route drilldown to show the top 5 model variants serving that route, token-level breakdown (prompt vs. generation vs. system tokens), and a cost attribution view that ties token segments to estimated cloud...

title: Latency Ribbon & Token Spark: per-model/per-route dashboard prototype and chart specs
preview: Goal: Design a compact per-model / per-route dashboard card and expanded view that surface latency distributions (p50/p95/p99), throughput (req/s), token metrics (tokens per request, tokens/sec), and an overlaid cost ribbon. The design must work as a small card in lists and a full exploratory canvas. Deliverables: F...
text: Goal: Design a compact per-model / per-route dashboard card and expanded view that surface latency distributions (p50/p95/p99), throughput (req/s), token metrics (tokens per request, tokens/sec), and an overlaid cost ribbon. The design must work as a small card in lists and a full exploratory canvas. Deliverables: Figma prototype, handoff-ready chart specs, accessibility notes, and a short usability script for 6 internal testers. This ticket covers the UX exploration and the first round of design reviews needed to reach engineering handoff.

title: Traffic Mosaic: Guided Canary Composer with Contextual Rollback Hints
preview: Design and prototype a new 'Traffic Mosaic' composer in the Redwood Console that lets engineers visually construct gradual canary and A/B traffic plans, preview live signal risk, and surfaces contextual rollback hints tied to telemetry thresholds. The mosaic metaphor groups cohorts as tiles (by region, customer segm...
text: Design and prototype a new 'Traffic Mosaic' composer in the Redwood Console that lets engineers visually construct gradual canary and A/B traffic plans, preview live signal risk, and surfaces contextual rollback hints tied to telemetry thresholds. The mosaic metaphor groups cohorts as tiles (by region, customer segment, or traffic signature) with inline controls for % allocation, schedule, and guardrail attachments. Key goals: reduce cognitive load when building multi-stage rollouts, make rollback options discoverable, and enable quick simulation of traffic paths under different latency/error profiles.

title: Establish clarity scale and press depth for buttons, tables, and surface shadows
preview: Problem: Our current token set has grown organically and surface depth (shadows/elevation) semantics vary between components. Buttons use a shallow inset on mobile but a heavier drop on desktop; table row hover/active states are inconsistent in depth and contrast; dense card layouts feel visually ambiguous in compac...
text:
Problem: Our current token set has grown organically and surface depth (shadows/elevation) semantics vary between components. Buttons use a shallow inset on mobile but a heavier drop on desktop; table row hover/active states are inconsistent in depth and contrast; dense card layouts feel visually ambiguous in compact mode. This creates cognitive friction for engineers implementing components and for product teams tuning density.

Goal: Create a small, well-documented clarity scale and a paired 'press depth' vocabulary that governs button depressions, table row activations, and card elevations. The result should be: 1) a reduced set of elevation tokens with explicit usage guidance, 2) accessible contrast bounds for each surface combination (background + elevation tint), 3) migration guidance and codemods for the component library, and 4) Figma components and CSS variables updated for c...

title: Changelog spotlight collage: compact before/after UI flows and annotated callouts
preview: Create a compact, reusable image pack for the upcoming release blog and changelog that highlights before/after Quickstart and Console onboarding flows. Deliver an annotated collage and a set of export-ready stills sized for blog hero, social, docs, and email. Focus is on clarity for product changes (new onboarding C...
text: Create a compact, reusable image pack for the upcoming release blog and changelog that highlights before/after Quickstart and Console onboarding flows. Deliver an annotated collage and a set of export-ready stills sized for blog hero, social, docs, and email. Focus is on clarity for product changes (new onboarding CTA, condensed stepper, and revised success state) while keeping file sizes small for CDN delivery. Deliverables: 1) Hero collage (desktop + mobile variants) showing side-by-side before/after flows with numbered callouts; 2) Three single-screen PNGs for key screens (welcome, quickstart flow, success modal); 3) Email header variant (600x200) and social preview (1200x628); 4) Editable Figma file with named components and export presets; 5) Accessibility notes (alt text, contrast ratios) and a short usage doc for Marketing/Docs. Constraints: work from current Console mocks in F...

title: Consolidate interaction shadow layers and type-ramp cadence for high-density components
preview:
Goal: unify shadow/elevation tokens used for interactive controls and tune the typography cadence so high-density surfaces (tables, compact forms, small buttons, and chart legends) remain legible and accessible without increasing vertical rhythm.

Scope:
- Introduce a small set of semantic interaction shadows (shado...
text:
Goal: unify shadow/elevation tokens used for interactive controls and tune the typography cadence so high-density surfaces (tables, compact forms, small buttons, and chart legends) remain legible and accessible without increasing vertical rhythm.

Scope:
- Introduce a small set of semantic interaction shadows (shadow.interactive.*) that replace multiple ad-hoc shadows on buttons, inputs, and chips.
- Revisit type ramp: tighten micro-scale steps for compact components and add a readable fallback for <12px usages.
- Adjust spacing/radius aliases for dense tables and compact buttons to reduce visual noise while preserving hit target guidance.
- Update chart token mappings for label contrast at 8px/10px sizes and micro-elevation for overlays/tooltips.

Why now: product teams report multiple visual regressions when switching density modes; engineering has trouble mapping component-only ove...

title: VPAT evidence pack + keyboard/focus polish for interactive data components
preview: Consolidate accessibility fixes and artifacts needed to complete a VPAT (Voluntary Product Accessibility Template) submission focused on dense data surfaces: tables, compact lists, interactive charts (SVG/canvas), and system buttons/token usage. This ticket covers: mapping current token contrast deltas, establishing...
text: Consolidate accessibility fixes and artifacts needed to complete a VPAT (Voluntary Product Accessibility Template) submission focused on dense data surfaces: tables, compact lists, interactive charts (SVG/canvas), and system buttons/token usage. This ticket covers: mapping current token contrast deltas, establishing keyboard focus/roving focus patterns for table rows and chart elements, ARIA attribute audit and pragmatic remediation for complex widgets, and producing the VPAT evidence pack (screenshots, code samples, keyboard walkthrough scripts). The goal is to unblock compliance review and provide clear, designer/engineer playbooks for consistent implementation across the component library.

title: Compact pivot lanes: multi-workspace wayfinding and cost-aware sidebar
preview: Problem: Customers navigating multiple workspaces and routes in the Redwood Console report difficulty locating per-workspace dashboards, rollouts, and cost breakdowns quickly. Current sidebar consumes too much vertical real-estate for users with many workspaces and offers weak affordances for routing between model-s...
text:
Problem: Customers navigating multiple workspaces and routes in the Redwood Console report difficulty locating per-workspace dashboards, rollouts, and cost breakdowns quickly. Current sidebar consumes too much vertical real-estate for users with many workspaces and offers weak affordances for routing between model-specific dashboards under different billing lenses.

Proposal: Introduce a compact "pivot lanes" pattern in the left rail. Pivot lanes are horizontal micro-rows inside an expandable left-rail group that represent orthogonal navigation dimensions: workspace, environment (hosted/dedicated/private), active cost-lens, and rollout stage. The default collapsed state shows one compact lane summary per dimension (icon + 2-word label + badge). Expanding a lane reveals quick-jump items and contextual filters (eg. model variant, region, allocation pool). The pattern aims to reduce vert...

title: per-session-token-attribution-deck-and-download-flow
preview: Design a compact, high-density token-level cost deck that surfaces per-session attribution, quick filters, and a frictionless export/download flow. The goal is to let engineers and product owners answer: which sessions, routes, and prompts drove the most token spend, how pricing slabs applied, and export slices for ...
text: Design a compact, high-density token-level cost deck that surfaces per-session attribution, quick filters, and a frictionless export/download flow. The goal is to let engineers and product owners answer: which sessions, routes, and prompts drove the most token spend, how pricing slabs applied, and export slices for billing or anomaly investigation. This ticket covers wireframes, an interactive Figma prototype, accessibility checks for tabular rows, and a small usability validation with 4 platform engineers.

title: Prompt-diff: cost forensics & variant compare pane
preview: Design an interactive 'Prompt-diff' pane in Redwood Console that lets engineers and PMs compare two or more prompt/route variants side-by-side to understand token-level cost delta and attribution. The pane targets use cases where teams are optimizing prompt wording, truncation, system message placement, or switching...
text: Design an interactive 'Prompt-diff' pane in Redwood Console that lets engineers and PMs compare two or more prompt/route variants side-by-side to understand token-level cost delta and attribution. The pane targets use cases where teams are optimizing prompt wording, truncation, system message placement, or switching model/quantization profiles and need immediate feedback on the cost implications and suggested pricing patterns to reduce unit economics. Deliverables: high-fidelity prototype for desktop Console, exportable comparison CSV/JSON, copy for help text, and a short motion spec for integration with Rollouts/A-B analysis.

title: Embed match onboarding visual tour and artifacts
preview: Create a visual-first onboarding tour and a set of product screenshots for the embedding + rerank flow. The deliverables will be used across the docs quickstart, in-console help, and a short in-app guided tour. Focus: a clear pipeline diagram that shows vector indexing, similarity search, reranker invocation, and fi...
text: Create a visual-first onboarding tour and a set of product screenshots for the embedding + rerank flow. The deliverables will be used across the docs quickstart, in-console help, and a short in-app guided tour. Focus: a clear pipeline diagram that shows vector indexing, similarity search, reranker invocation, and final ranked output; sample outputs with token highlights; and compact annotated screenshots for the Console onboarding card.

title: Elastic breakpoints and overflow handling for dense Console surfaces (dashboards, rollouts, cost views)
preview: Objective: Implement an 'elastic breakpoints' system and overflow-handling patterns that keep data-dense Console pages (dashboards, rollouts, cost views) readable and interactive across viewport sizes and browsers. This ticket covers design decisions, prototypes, acceptance criteria, and cross-browser compatibility ...
text: Objective: Implement an 'elastic breakpoints' system and overflow-handling patterns that keep data-dense Console pages (dashboards, rollouts, cost views) readable and interactive across viewport sizes and browsers. This ticket covers design decisions, prototypes, acceptance criteria, and cross-browser compatibility notes for handoff to engineering. Motivation: We are seeing clipping, awkward wrapping, and scroll-jank on smaller viewports and on Safari/Firefox when multiple dense components (tables + charts + modals) appear together. The goal is a coherent set of layout rules and component-level fallbacks that prioritize information hierarchy, keyboard navigation, and low-latency reflow without heavy JS.

title: Token Footprint Lenses & Exportable Pricing Tiles
preview: Problem statement: Customers want a compact, exportable view that lets platform engineers and finance teams understand token-level cost drivers across routes, roles, and time. Existing mockups are either too dense for quick ops triage or too sparse for chargeback. This ticket designs a set of reusable "lenses" — sma...
text:
Problem statement: Customers want a compact, exportable view that lets platform engineers and finance teams understand token-level cost drivers across routes, roles, and time. Existing mockups are either too dense for quick ops triage or too sparse for chargeback. This ticket designs a set of reusable "lenses" — small focused panels — and a tiling system (pricing tiles) that can be rearranged and exported as CSV/JSON/PDF. The deliverable is a high-fidelity prototype, interaction spec, and annotated export contract for engineering.

Scope:
- Token-level breakdown by model, route, customer tag, and time window.
- Attribution view that ties tokens to request type + prompt template + caching behavior.
- Export formats: CSV for finance ingest, JSON schema for programmatic audits, printable PDF for exec reports.
- Tile library with 6 variants (Summary, Timeline, Hotspots, Attribution Heatma...

title: Harmonize focus states, color contrast and i18n behavior across Cost View and Rollouts dashboards
preview: Overview: The Cost View and Rollouts dashboards currently have inconsistent focus indicators, marginal color contrast for secondary data labels, and localized layout breakage for several locales (notably fr-FR and ar-SA). This ticket drives a consolidated design solution and prototype to: 1) standardize keyboard foc...
text: Overview: The Cost View and Rollouts dashboards currently have inconsistent focus indicators, marginal color contrast for secondary data labels, and localized layout breakage for several locales (notably fr-FR and ar-SA). This ticket drives a consolidated design solution and prototype to: 1) standardize keyboard focus and skip navigation for primary dashboard controls; 2) update color token usage to meet WCAG AA/AAA where appropriate for quantitative labels; 3) validate number/date formatting and label wrapping for LTR and RTL languages; and 4) provide annotated specs and test cases for engineering QA and localization teams.

title: Caretaker Protocol — Component Longevity Framework
preview: Establish a repeatable, lightweight governance pattern for maintaining design tokens and core components (buttons, tables, charts) that balances stability for product teams with the ability to iterate. This ticket captures the proposed Caretaker Protocol: a definition of ownership, an RFC cadence for non-trivial cha...
text: Establish a repeatable, lightweight governance pattern for maintaining design tokens and core components (buttons, tables, charts) that balances stability for product teams with the ability to iterate. This ticket captures the proposed Caretaker Protocol: a definition of ownership, an RFC cadence for non-trivial changes, a compact review checklist, automated signals for component health, and a clear deprecation pathway.

title: Atomic component inventory, interaction-contrast playbook, and Figma tokens export
preview: Consolidate a single-source atomic component inventory and produce an interaction-contrast playbook that pairs with an exportable Figma token strategy. The goal is to remove ad-hoc variations in dashboard widgets, rollout rails, and cost cards; provide explicit contrast/hover/focus rules; and produce a clean tokens ...
text: Consolidate a single-source atomic component inventory and produce an interaction-contrast playbook that pairs with an exportable Figma token strategy. The goal is to remove ad-hoc variations in dashboard widgets, rollout rails, and cost cards; provide explicit contrast/hover/focus rules; and produce a clean tokens export that engineering can consume for console-2026.04 rollout.

title: Stackable component wrap + layout reflow anomalies on Safari/Firefox mobile — aggregation and prioritized QA
preview:
Problem statement:
Multiple regression reports from QA and customer support indicate that certain 'stackable' components (horizontal token stacks, responsive button groups, compact table rows, and chart legend chips) unexpectedly overflow, reflow, or collapse into a single-line on constrained viewports when used in ...
text:
Problem statement:
Multiple regression reports from QA and customer support indicate that certain 'stackable' components (horizontal token stacks, responsive button groups, compact table rows, and chart legend chips) unexpectedly overflow, reflow, or collapse into a single-line on constrained viewports when used in Safari on iOS and Firefox on Android. The behavior is inconsistent across breakpoints and sometimes linked to momentum scrolling or delayed font fallback. This ticket will centralize findings, record reliable repro steps, propose short-term mitigations, and create engineering tickets for long-term fixes.

Scope:
- Reproduce and classify failures into deterministic and timing-dependent categories.
- Produce minimal test cases (static HTML + component instances) for each category.
- Ship a short-lived CSS patch and a token spacing tweak for the next minor UI Kit release to re...

title: Global latency corridors — onboarding visual canvas & screenshot assets
preview: Create a set of onboarding/quickstart visuals and accompanying product screenshots that explain Redwood's region and latency routing behavior. Deliverables include an accessible global map canvas, a step-through animated storyboard (6 frames) for the quickstart tour, console screenshot overlays demonstrating routing...
text: Create a set of onboarding/quickstart visuals and accompanying product screenshots that explain Redwood's region and latency routing behavior. Deliverables include an accessible global map canvas, a step-through animated storyboard (6 frames) for the quickstart tour, console screenshot overlays demonstrating routing decisions, and export-ready SVG/PNG/MP4 assets for docs and product release notes.

title: Model Lineage Traffic Steward — compact wireflow for multi-model routing and rollback
preview: Goal: Prototype a compact rollout composer focused on multi-model traffic steering and per-variant lineage inspection. The flow should let engineers and PMs direct traffic across model variants (e.g., quantized / float32 / distilled), create conditional routing rules by request attributes, preview model lineage for ...
text: Goal: Prototype a compact rollout composer focused on multi-model traffic steering and per-variant lineage inspection. The flow should let engineers and PMs direct traffic across model variants (e.g., quantized / float32 / distilled), create conditional routing rules by request attributes, preview model lineage for sampled requests, and execute fast rollback at cohort or model-variant granularity. Emphasis: clear foreground actions for emergency snapback, explainable traces to audit which model served which sample, and a simulation mode that forecasts metric drift (latency, cost/unit, regression rate) before applying weights.

title: Component Evolution Guidelines: Tokens → Buttons → Tables → Charts
preview: Create an operational, lightweight playbook for evolving Redwood's design system components and tokens so product teams can contribute safely and platform teams can retire or modify variants without breaking apps. This covers authoring contributions (tokens, buttons, tables, charts), the review checklist for design ...
text: Create an operational, lightweight playbook for evolving Redwood's design system components and tokens so product teams can contribute safely and platform teams can retire or modify variants without breaking apps. This covers authoring contributions (tokens, buttons, tables, charts), the review checklist for design + engineering, a minimal RFC flow for large changes, and a pragmatic deprecation/retirement policy that balances migration effort with product stability.

title: Incident Worksheet: Structured Error Triage & Retry Heuristics for Console
preview: Design and prototype an Incident Worksheet canvas inside the Redwood Console that guides engineers through structured error triage for failed inference requests. The worksheet synthesizes telemetry, payload diffs, KV/cache hints, model variant fallbacks, and retry heuristics into a concise, actionable UI. Goal is to...
text: Design and prototype an Incident Worksheet canvas inside the Redwood Console that guides engineers through structured error triage for failed inference requests. The worksheet synthesizes telemetry, payload diffs, KV/cache hints, model variant fallbacks, and retry heuristics into a concise, actionable UI. Goal is to reduce mean time to resolution (MTTR) for failed request classes and make rollout rollback decisions (canary vs full) more confident. Target personas: SRE/ML Engineer on-call, Platform engineer running Dedicated clusters, and PM owning SLAs. Key screens: 1) Headline summary with failure taxonomy and cost delta; 2) Timeline + sequence map of retries & fallbacks; 3) Payload diff & ephemeral KV snapshot with redaction controls; 4) Retry rules visualizer with suggested playbook actions (backoff adjustments, model variant fallback, temporary routing); 5) Quick actions panel inc...

title: GPU Hub Minimap & Autoscale Gestalt — compact controls and triage overlay
preview:
Goal: Design a compact, high-density dashboard module (GPU Hub Minimap) for the Dedicated Capacity console that surfaces pool health, autoscaling posture, and rapid triage controls in a single glance. The module must work as a tiled card in the fleet overview and as a focused overlay for incident response.

Why this...
text:
Goal: Design a compact, high-density dashboard module (GPU Hub Minimap) for the Dedicated Capacity console that surfaces pool health, autoscaling posture, and rapid triage controls in a single glance. The module must work as a tiled card in the fleet overview and as a focused overlay for incident response.

Why this exists: Customers operating Dedicated GPU pools need a fast, low-noise way to detect emergent latency/cost regressions and take immediate mitigations (burst enable, drain, fallback route) without switching pages. Current fleet pages are too verbose for on-call flow; this ticket explores a condensed gestalt that preserves actionability.

Primary users: SREs, infra/platform engineers, customer success who perform triage or quick tuning on Dedicated pools.

Design constraints and guiding principles:
- Compactness: fit key signals into a 320x200 card while remaining readable o...
